channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: People are using Strava to create amazing street art
item.description: Strava, the fitness tracking app used by more than 120 million athletes around the world, is being used by a handful of hobbyists to create amazing works of art. Canadian accountant Duncan McCabe is one of those choosing to do more than track how often he pounds the sidewalk on regular runs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bluesky has a growing problem: fake accounts
item.description: On Wednesday afternoon, billionaire activist investor Bill Ackman appeared to post on Bluesky a plan to destroy the burgeoning social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Apple readies more conversational Siri in bid to catch up in AI
item.description: Apple has been testing the upgraded software on iPhones, iPads and Macs as a separate app, but the technology will ultimately replace the Siri interface that users rely on today.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Windows 11 will soon be available on Meta Quest 3 headsets
item.description: Meta Quest 3 and Quest 3S headset owners will soon gain access to the “full capabilities” of Windows 11 in mixed reality, Microsoft announced at its Ignite conference this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What is a data architect? Skills, salaries, and how to become a data framework master
item.description: Data architect role Data architects are senior visionaries who translate business requirements into technology requirements, and define data standards and principles, often in support of data or digital transformations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australia rejects Elon Musk's claim that it plans to control access to the internet
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister has rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intends to control all Australians' access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gary Gensler said he’ll step down as SEC chair. Bitcoin is soaring
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are ecstatic after U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chair Gary Gensler said he will step down from his post on January 20 once President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: AI discrimination lawsuit reaches $2.2 million settlement
item.description: Mary Louis’s excitement to move into an apartment in Massachusetts in the spring of 2021 turned to dismay when Louis, a Black woman, received an email saying that a “third-party service” had denied her tenancy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Red Hat Linux to be official WSL distro
item.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) is set to become an official Linux distribution for Microsoft’s Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL), a Windows feature that allows users to run Linux environments on Windows without a separate virtual machine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple Pay, other tech firms come under CFPB regulatory oversight
item.description: CFPB can already take action against companies that break the law, the new rule would allow the CFPB to regularly supervise the large digital-wallet and payments firms and their practices.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: EU industry group has its eye on Microsoft’s cloud business
item.description: Microsoft’s cloud business is under surveillance in Europe: The association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) has set up new monitoring body to ensure the tech giant complies with a legal settlement to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft rebrands Azure AI Studio to Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Microsoft is packaging its Azure AI Studio and other updates into a new service — Azure AI Foundry in response to enterprises’ need to develop, run, and manage generative AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 14 great preprocessors for developers who love to code
item.description: As much as we love them, programming languages can often feel like straitjackets. They’re a complex bundle of syntactic rules, and if we break them—even once—the compiler starts screaming out error messages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation
